beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make espresso at home
beats.reasoning: Viktor is a night owl but full energy today; morning routine is quick and efficient. He refuses drip coffee, so he'll make espresso at home before heading out.

beats.activity: Drive from Leschi to Georgetown clinic
beats.reasoning: Early commute to work; Georgetown is south and accessible by car avoiding Light Rail closure (which only affects Westlake–U-District). Sunny weather makes the drive pleasant.

beats.activity: Morning patient sessions at the sports clinic
beats.reasoning: Viktor works as a physical therapist; morning shift covers typical clinic hours before the USA–Wales match airs at 11am.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match at a downtown bar
beats.reasoning: The match airs at 11am; Viktor is a future-oriented planner and will shift his schedule to catch the live event at a bar downtown. Kremwerk is a popular spot and he values the social atmosphere over solitude. Light Rail closure doesn't affect car/bus access to Capitol Hill.

beats.activity: Lunch at a Capitol Hill café
beats.reasoning: Post-match social lunch; staying in Capitol Hill to grab food nearby. Sunny 74F weather makes outdoor dining appealing.
Return to work for afternoon shift; Light Rail closure is still in effect (9am–3pm) but doesn't affect southbound driving.

beats.activity: Afternoon patient sessions and clinic admin
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon work block; clinic closes by late afternoon. Viktor finishes before evening leisure time.

beats.activity: Drive from clinic to climbing gym
beats.reasoning: Viktor spends free time on rock climbing; Vertical World is in Georgetown, minimizing travel time. The sunny 74F weather and full energy make evening climbing appealing.
Core leisure activity for Viktor; evening climbing session before heading home. Good weather and post-work energy make this an ideal time.

beats.activity: Drive from climbing gym to home
beats.reasoning: Return to Leschi after climbing; evening commute is straightforward and completes the work–exercise loop.

diary: Woke up early with good energy—the weather was perfect for it. Quick espresso at home, then headed down to the clinic in Georgetown. Morning flew by with back-to-back sessions; one runner with a torn meniscus was finally showing real progress, which felt good. Around mid-morning I realized the USA-Wales match was starting, and honestly, I couldn't resist. Drove up to Kremwerk in Capitol Hill and caught the whole thing with a crowd of rowdy fans. The energy was infectious. Grabbed lunch at Molly Moon's afterward—their ice cream hit different on a day like this. Headed back to finish afternoon sessions, then straight to Vertical World to burn off the restless feeling. Climbing always clears my head. By the time I got home, I was pleasantly exhausted.
thought_process: The weather and the World Cup vibe really pulled me in today. I almost never take a long lunch break, but I felt this pull to be around people and energy—maybe because it's early summer and everyone's mood shifts. The Light Rail closure didn't even factor in since I drive, so that was a non-issue. I think I chose to go to the match because the clinic schedule was manageable enough, and honestly, I needed that break. Work's been solid but repetitive. Climbing at the end felt like the right capstone—physical exertion after sitting in bars and cars. My mood lifted noticeably once I got outside and moved my body, which is pretty typical for me.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
